text.title: Team AI Usage Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-usage-policy-team methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Policy MUST contain exactly three concrete lists: always-OK (≥5 items), never-OK (≥5 items), ask-first (≥3 items). Each item is a concrete action, not a principle.
rule.rationale: Abstract 'follow company policy' guidance is ignored; concrete lists are skimmable in 60 seconds.

rule.statement: Policy MUST have a named owner (single person + role) and a quarterly review date. Reviews not held by the deadline auto-flag the policy as stale.
rule.rationale: Unowned policies decay; quarterly cadence is the minimum for AI tooling evolution.

rule.statement: Policy MUST include a 4-step incident-response runbook: detect → contain → notify → learn. Each step has a named role and a time-bound action.
rule.rationale: Without IR runbook, accidental PII paste becomes a multi-hour scramble; codified steps cut median containment time.

rule.statement: The ask-first list MUST name a single decision-maker (role + Slack channel + 24h SLA) for any case not covered by always-OK / never-OK lists.
rule.rationale: Without explicit gray-zone resolution, devs default to either over-broad allow or over-conservative block; both erode trust.

rule.statement: Rendered policy MUST fit on one screen / page (≤ 500 words). Long policies are not read.
rule.rationale: Behavioural research: policies > 1 page have < 20% read-completion rate.
